articles.title: Oil Rebounds as US Hits Hormuz Targets With Talks at an Impasse
articles.summary: Oil advanced, following a drop of more than 5% on Wednesday, as US forces made fresh strikes against Iran, while Washington and Tehran remained at odds over how to reopen the Strait of Hormuz.

articles.title: Chinese Coal Disaster to Ripple Through Steel, Power, Chemicals
articles.summary: The Shanxi coal disaster is likely to hit Chinese output in the short term, raising costs for steelmakers, power plants and chemicals manufacturers.

articles.title: Dollar-Denominated EM Assets Outperforming: UBS' Khan
articles.summary: Shamaila Khan, UBS Asset Management Head of Global EM and APAC Fixed Income, speaks with Bloomberg TV about the strong opportunities in dollar-denominated emerging market assets. (Source: Bloomberg)

articles.title: Gold Extends Losses as US Strikes Dampen Iran Peace Talk Hopes
articles.summary: Gold extended a two-day decline as fresh US airstrikes on an Iranian military base risked derailing peace talks and kept inflation risks high.

articles.title: Palm Oil Titan Wilmar Dives Most in Six Years on Indonesia Probe
articles.summary: Wilmar International Ltd. fell the most in almost six years after the Indonesian government named the palm oil titan as one of the companies being probed for suspected export abuses.

articles.title: Asian Stocks Slip, Oil Gains on Fresh Iran Strikes: Markets Wrap
articles.summary: Asian equities eased from all-time highs after conflicting signals from the US and Iran on prospects for a deal to end the war. Crude oil gained.

articles.title: Nomura Analyst Says Near-Term BOJ Hike Still Up in Air on Iran
articles.summary: Market indicators suggest that traders are nearly convinced that the Bank of Japan will raise interest rates in June or July, but a Nomura group analyst isn’t so sure.

articles.title: China Markets $885 Million Green Sovereign Bond in Hong Kong
articles.summary: China is selling as much as 6 billion yuan ($885 million) of green sovereign bonds in Hong Kong on Thursday, returning to the international ESG debt market a year after its debut in London.

articles.title: China’s Declining Appetite for Oil Laid Bare by Iran War
articles.summary: Chinese imports of crude oil are set to drop to levels not seen since the pandemic, as the war in Iran reveals the extent to which demand has disappeared and may not be coming back.

articles.title: Google Engineer Charged With Insider Trading on Polymarket
articles.summary: A Google software engineer was charged with insider trading on Polymarket, where he allegedly made more than $1 million betting on one of last year’s most popular Internet searches.
